title: Authentication
text: essence three authentication methods used package CDSE: Client ID Client Secret User identifier User password S3 key + secret access tokens long pieces encrypted codes can request specific secrets associated CDSE account. access tokens valid short period time (usually 10 minutes), may need obtain fresh token .

title: Authentication
text: list shows three authentication methods required functions package: dse_get_token_details() decoding token dse_set_gdal_token() making token visible GDAL driver dse_usage() dse_user_statistics() dse_sh_features() dse_sh_process() dse_sh_search_request() dse_sh_use_requests_builder() dse_sh_features() dse_get_token_details() decoding token dse_odata_download_path() dse_stac_download() case https download dse_stac_download() case S3 download dse_odata_download() dse_s3_download()

title: Authentication
text: authentication methods share commonality: require CDSE user account. can create one visiting login page click “register”. Fill form follow instructions.

title: Authentication
text: completing registration user id (usually e-mail) password (created ). can use obtain public access token, can use functions listed . can obtain token calling dse_public_access_token(), user Id password.

title: Authentication
text: Getting (non-public) access token, need complete additional steps. order retrieve key, need register client. register client, visit Sentinel Dashboard go “User settings”. option create OAuth client. Follow instructions, make sure safely copy client id secret. latter displayed . detailed instructions provided API documentation can now retrieve access token (required functions listed ) calling dse_access_token() client Id client secret created steps .

title: Authentication
text: create S3 key secret, visit s3-credentials page, log CDSE account details. clicking “add credential”, can create new key secret. Store safe place, secret shown . can pass key secret s3_key s3_secret arguments functions requesting (see ).

title: Authentication
text: might want share R scripts use CopernicusDataspace package, don’t want share personal secret information, way around including script. can store system environment variables, automatically detected package. can set Sys.setenv() start R session. can also edit .renviron, loaded automatically new R session. easiest way calling usethis::edit_r_environ() add following lines. Obviously, need replace text <> personal details. new R session, environment variables available, longer pass secret information arguments functions. obtained environment.
code: CDSE_API_USERNAME="<your_CDSE_username>" CDSE_API_PASSWORD="<your_CDSE_password>" CDSE_API_CLIENTID="<your_client_id>" CDSE_API_CLIENTSECRET="<your_client_secret>" CDSE_API_S3ID="<your_s3_key>" CDSE_API_S3SECRET="<your_s3_secret>"

title: Authentication
text: important note functions dse_access_token() dse_public_access_token() wrapped memoise::memoise(). means function result stored memory function called , previous results reused. One advantage speeds operations, multiple sequential calls need access tokens. also prevent rate limiting actions, server might block accessively request tokens. disadvantage token might expire, old token reused due memoise strategy. happens, can wipe memoised token calling memoise::forget(dse_access_token) memoise::forget(dse_public_access_token), respectively. get fresh token calling token functions.

title: Using the OData API
text: searching products, simply use dse_odata_products(), directly add filters arguments. sophisticated queries, can use dse_odata_products_request(). creates special class httr2 request object. essence, request API server, can modify tidyverse operators. sounds complicated . created request, can add tidyverse operators (like filter(), arrange() slice_head()), modify request. can join modifications pipe operator (|> %>%). can also query products intersect specific spatial features (sf) using st_intersects(). collection specific fields can use filter product search. discover fields use, call dse_odata_attributes(). shown example , filter products created January first 2025. image overlaps bounding box specify. create descending arrangement identifiers, select first 5 results. calling collect() ensure request performed result returned human readable form (data.frame).
code: library(CopernicusDataspace) library(dplyr) # Tidiverse package for data manipulation library(sf)    # package for handling simple (spatial) features  ## Define a bounding box for our search query: bbox <-   st_bbox(     c(xmin = 5.261, ymin = 52.680, xmax = 5.319, ymax = 52.715),     crs = 4326) |>     st_as_sfc()  ## Create an API request: dse_odata_products_request() |>   ## Filter content on ContentDate/Start`:   filter(     `ContentDate/Start` > "2025-01-01") |>   ## Only data that intersects with our bounding box:   st_intersects(bbox) |>   ## Arrange by descending Id:   arrange(desc(Id)) |>   ## Only the first 5 hits:   slice_head(n = 5) |>   ## Collect the results:   collect()

title: Using the OData API
text: Bursts concept associated Sentinel-1 C-SAR instrument. dedicated functions searching specific burst data: dse_odata_bursts() dse_odata_bursts_request(). equivalents dse_odata_products() dse_odata_products_request(). work mechanisms downloading works normal OData products, shown . basic example looking downloading burst data:
code: burst_req <-   dse_odata_bursts_request(ParentProductId == "879d445c-2c67-5b30-8589-b1f478904269")  ## Note that these are large files and may take a while to download: dse_odata_download(   burst_req,   tempdir() )

title: Using the OData API
text: identified interesting product, ways explore . need product identifier (ID) . dse_odata_product_nodes() can see files associated product: available can also get quick peek product calling dse_odata_quicklook():
code: dse_odata_product_nodes("c8ed8edb-9bef-4717-abfd-1400a57171a4",                         recursive = TRUE) dse_odata_quicklook(     "91822f33-b15c-5b60-aa39-6d9f6f5c773b",     tempfile(fileext = ".jpg"))

title: Using the OData API
text: shown ‘exploring’ section , products can contain ‘nodes’, either represent file directory. want download files directory can call dse_odata_download_path(). download files specified node path. demonstrated : example uses alternative https route requires private access token, turn requires Copernicus Dataspace user id (usually e-mail address) password (see vignette("Authentication")). formal OData route uses [S3 buckets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_S3 requires S3 authentication (see vignette("Authentication")). can perform downloads dse_odata_downloads() shown
code: if (dse_has_password()) {   dse_odata_download_path(     product     = "2f497806-0101-5eea-83fa-c8f68bc56b0c",     node_path   =       paste("DEM1_SAR_DTE_90_20101213T034716_20130408T035028_ADS_000000_5033.DEM",             "Copernicus_DSM_30_S09_00_E026_00", "DEM",             "Copernicus_DSM_30_S09_00_E026_00_DEM.dt1", sep = "/"),     destination = tempdir()     ) } if (dse_has_s3_secret()) {   dse_odata_download(       dse_odata_products(         Name ==           "S1A_IW_OCN__2SDH_20250707T210608_20250707T210625_059983_07739B_893E.SAFE"),       destination = tempdir()) }

title: Using the STAC Catalogue
text: good starting point exploring data STAC aware collections data available first place. can list follows: returned data.frame contains descriptive information collections. can help focus search. identified collection, can check filter/search available narrowing exploration tour: example shows 11 properties can used focus search. can start actual search creating STAC search request : dse_stac_search_request(). creates special class httr2 request object. essence, request API server, can modify tidyverse operators. sounds complicated . created request, can add tidyverse operators (like filter(), arrange() slice_head()), modify request. can join modifications pipe operator (|> %>%). can also query products intersect specific spatial features (sf) using st_intersects().
code: library(CopernicusDataspace) dse_stac_collections() dse_stac_queryables("sentinel-1-grd") |> summary() library(dplyr) library(sf)  bbox <-   sf::st_bbox(     c(xmin = 5.261, ymin = 52.680, xmax = 5.319, ymax = 52.715),     crs = 4326)  dse_stac_search_request("sentinel-1-grd") |>   filter(`sat:orbit_state` == "ascending") |>   arrange("id") |>   st_intersects(bbox) |>   collect()

title: Using the STAC Catalogue
text: downloading data, retrieve Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) dse_stac_get_uri(). get URI, need least asset identifier specific asset. can obtained search shown . example shows : approach also needs collection asset made available. don’t provide , guessed dse_stac_guess_collection(). function 100% reliable, ’s best practice provide collection manually. Instead working URI easier call dse_stac_download(). automatically takes care required authentication downloading file (properly provided). Check vignette("Authentication") information authentication process.
code: dse_stac_get_uri(   asset_id = "S2A_MSIL1C_20260109T132741_N0511_R024_T39XVL_20260109T142148",   asset = "B01",   collection = "sentinel-2-l1c" ) dse_stac_download(   asset_id = "S2A_MSIL1C_20260109T132741_N0511_R024_T39XVL_20260109T142148",   asset = "B01",   collection = "sentinel-2-l1c",   destination = tempdir() )

title: Using SentinelHub
text: good point start using SentinelHub listing collections available service. can also search products using SentinelHub creating dse_sh_search_request(). can add narrow search tidyverse filter(). need retrieve search results calling collect():
code: library(CopernicusDataspace) library(stars) ## required below for reading and plotting data library(dplyr) ## used for data manipulation below dse_sh_collections() bounds <- c(5.261, 52.680, 5.319, 52.715) if (dse_has_client_info()) {   dse_sh_search_request(     collection = "sentinel-2-l2a",     bbox       = bounds,     datetime   = c("2025-01-01 UTC", "2025-01-31 UTC")   ) |>     filter(`eo:cloud_cover` <= 10) |>     collect() }

text: SentinelHub processing service, meaning attempt downloading raw data. title: Reading Data
text: cases data stored raster data, orientation satellite made observation. data may valid Coordinate Reference System (CRS), instead Ground Control Points (GCP), geo-reference data. spatial analyses, first need warp data meaningful projection. data masks preferably applied raw data warps (require interpolation). Let’s demonstrate step--step user-case. product Id, specific collection, bounding box: area interest. first obtain Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) S3 service product (dse_stac_get_uri()). convert URI Virtual System Interface (VSI) using dse_s3_uri_to_vsi(). can directly connect product using VSI stars::read_stars(). Note need run dse_s3_set_gdal_options() first, make S3 secrets available GDAL driver used stars package. create proxy raster object. Meaning data transferred unless necessary. allows us lazily specify operations raster object, even downloaded. steps shown . working stars package demonstration, acknowledge package preserve GCPs manipulating raster. Therefore, explicitly read information original product, can later rely . raw data geo-referenced, can also use GCP information subset raw data area interest. order reduce data transfer computational effort.  product contains two bands (VH VV). bands share geometry use GCPs. Therefore, can use VV band mask VH band, use VH values VV measurements least 30 dB. Calling stars::write_stars() force lazy operations executed require data downloaded stored specified location. VH band now masked. plotted, notice (familiar area) image mirrored. shown raster index coordinates.  process masking, sub-setting downloading data, GCPs got lost. first need reintroduce , can continue. Now GCPs reintroduced, can warp image relevant CRS. case create 10m x 10m grid UTM31N projection.
code: if (dse_has_s3_secret()) {   ## Define the area of interest:   bbox <- st_bbox(c(xmin =  4.66,                     ymin = 52.842,                     xmax =  7.15,                     ymax = 53.62), crs = 4326)      ## Assume that we have already located the asset below within our   ## area of interest:   id <- "S1A_IW_GRDH_1SDV_20241125T055820_20241125T055845_056707_06F55C_12F9_COG"      ## And it's from this collection:   cllctn <- "sentinel-1-grd"      ## Get the URI for the manifest.safe file. This is the preferred entry   ## point into the data. It contains all bands and all meta-information   ## (like accurate Ground Control Points):   uri_safe <- dse_stac_get_uri(id, "safe_manifest", cllctn)      ## Translate the URI into a virtual system indicator (VSI), such that   ## the stars package can interact with it:   vsi_safe <- dse_s3_uri_to_vsi(uri_safe)      ## Set up the S3 credentials such that the stars package (and its   ## underpinning GDAL library) will recognise them.   dse_s3_set_gdal_options()      ## Create a stars proxy object from the VSI. No data is downloaded   ## yet. But you can set up lazy data manipulations with tidyverse.   proxy <- read_stars(vsi_safe, proxy = TRUE) } ## Use `gdal_info` to retrieve the raster information json_info <-   gdal_utils("info", vsi_safe, options = c("-json"), quiet = TRUE) |>   fromJSON()  ## Get the ground control points for the specific raster. ## You will need it for subsetting the proxy. gcp <-   st_as_sf(json_info$gcps$gcpList, coords= c("x", "y"), crs =               st_crs(json_info$gcps$coordinateSystem$wkt))  ## A plot showing where the raster's GCPs are, and where the bbox lies par(mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1)) plot(gcp["pixel"] |> st_geometry(), main = "Ground Control Points", col = NA) plot(gcp["pixel"], add = TRUE) plot(bbox |> st_as_sfc() |> st_geometry(), border = "red", add = TRUE)  ## Determine which raster indices are within the bbox ## of our area of interest: gcp_crop <-   st_crop(gcp,           st_transform(bbox |>                          st_as_sfc() |>                          st_buffer(units::as_units(1, "km")),                        st_crs(json_info$gcps$coordinateSystem$wkt)))  dim_x <- st_get_dimension_values(proxy, "x") |> sort() dim_x <- which(dim_x >= min(gcp_crop$pixel) & dim_x <= max(gcp_crop$pixel)) dim_y <- st_get_dimension_values(proxy, "y") |> sort() dim_y <- which(dim_y >= min(gcp_crop$line) & dim_y <= max(gcp_crop$line)) masked_proxy <-   proxy[,dim_x, dim_y,] |>   st_apply(c("x", "y"), \(x) {     ## From json_info$bands we know that band 1=VH and 2=VV     ## We want VH but only when VV >= 30:     ifelse(x[2] >= 30, x[1], NA)   })  ## Store the masked file: masked_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".tif") write_stars(masked_proxy, masked_file, chunk_size = c(1024, 1024),             NA_value = -9999) masked <- read_stars(masked_file) plot(masked, main = "masked") ## The masked file has lost original GCP information ## So create a reference file where we explicitly add the GCP info ref_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".tif")  ## Update GCP info for selected raster indices: gcp <-   json_info$gcps$gcpList |>   mutate(pixel = pixel - min(dim_x) - 1,          line = line - min(dim_y) - 1) |>   filter(line >= 0 & pixel >= 0) opts <-   cbind("-gcp", as.matrix(gcp[, c("pixel", "line", "x", "y", "x")])) |>   apply(1, c) |> c()  ## Create reference file with GCP info gdal_utils(   "translate",   source = masked_file,   destination = ref_file,   options = c(opts,               "-a_srs", json_info$gcps$coordinateSystem$wkt,               "-a_nodata", "-9999") ## missing values ) ## Warp the masked file to a fixed raster with meaningful CRS ## (In this case UTM31 with 10x10m cells) warped_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".tif")  gdal_utils(   "warp",   source = ref_file,   destination = warped_file,   options = c(     "-t_srs", "EPSG:32631",     "-tps",     "-tr", c(10, 10), "-tap", "-r", "bilinear",     "-srcnodata", "-9999", ## missing values     "-dstnodata", "-9999", ## missing values     "-overwrite")) warped <- read_stars(warped_file)  plot(warped, reset = FALSE, axes = TRUE, main = "warped")
